Fig. 3 Spatial distribution of industrial heat source objects", "# An Industrial Heat Source Extraction Method: BP Neural Network Using Temperature Feature Template", "Abstract: Aiming at the problem of insufficient quantity and spatial refinement in the extraction of industrial heat source from annual scale thermal anomaly data, a neural network industrial heat source extraction method based on temperature feature template is proposed by using VIIRS active fire data. This study took Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas as the study area, Firstly, according to the spatial aggregation characteristics of industrial heat sources, the heat source objects were divided by the OPTICS algorithm. Secondly, according to the thermal radiation characteristics of the heat sources, the temperature characteristic template of industrial heat sources and non- industrial heat sources were constructed. Finally, the BP neural network was used to extract industrial heat source objects using the temperature feature template and heat source statistical characteristics as parameters. The results show that: (1) the extraction precision of industrial heat source of the neural network algorithm of temperature feature template proposed in this paper reached  $96.31\%$ . Compared with time filtering and logistic regression methods, the extraction precision of industrial heat sources was improved by  $8.45\%$  and  $7.53\%$ , respectively; (2) From 2015 to 2020, the number of industrial heat sources in the six provinces and cities in Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas decreased by  $27.46\%$ . The number of industrial heat source objects and heat anomalies in Hebei Province decreased by  $8.06\%$  and  $7.44\%$  annually, respectively, which was the largest decrease compared with other provinces and cities. The", "concentration of industrial heat sources in Shandong and Tianjin increased by  $25.72\%$  and  $86.64\%$ , respectively, indicating that the industrial transformation and upgrade policies in the two places have achieved remarkable results; (3) Tangshan, Handan, Lvliang, and Changzhi accounted for  $31.37\%$  of the total industrial heat sources in the study area, which are the main cities in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas. The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in seven cities such as Linfen and Taiyuan was higher than those in other cities; The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in 11 cities such as Beijing and Zhoukou was lower than those in other cities; (4) From January to May 2020, the number of industrial heat anomalies in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas remained unchanged or increased compared with the same period in 2019 and 2021. The COVID-19 had no significant impact on the industrial heat source in the study area. The number of industrial heat anomalies in Wuhan in January and February 2020 decreased by more than  $66.67\%$  compared with that in the same period in 2019 and 2021, the number of industrial heat anomalies from March to May 2020 was lower than that in the same period of 2019. The COVID-19 has had a significant impact on industrial heat sources in Wuhan from January to May 2020. This study reflects the current situation and trend of industrial heat source development in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas, which provides a valuable reference for the formulation and adjustment of relevant policies such as reducing energy consumption and improving secondary industry concentration.", "Key words: industrial heat source; OPTICS algorithm; temperature feature template; neural network; VIIRS Active Fire; Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas", "图1基于BP神经网络的工业热源对象提取方法技术流程

Seasonal spatial distribution of the LAI in the model domain.", "supplement": "(a).spring;(b).summer;(c).autumn;(d).winter", "references": ["MEGAN v2.1 (Guenther et al. 2012) was utilized to estimate BVOC emissions in China with a  $27\mathrm{-km}$  horizontal grid spacing domain (shown in Fig. 1). The inputs for MEGAN include the LAI, PFTs, emission factors, and meteorological data (e.g., solar radiation, temperature, relative humidity and soil moisture). The monthly average LAI was obtained from the 8-day MODIS LAI product (MOD15A2, 2017) with the same horizontal resolution as the study domain, as shown in Fig. 2. The PFT map was obtained from the MODIS MCD12Q1 product and regridded into the WRF domain to compute the fraction of each PFT in each grid (Fig. 3). The default MEGAN emission factors  $(\mathrm{mg~m}^{-2}\mathrm{h}^{-1})$  with a resolution of  $30\mathrm{s}$ $(< 1\mathrm{km})$ , which reflect the BVOC emission rate under standard canopy conditions, were leveraged (http://lar.wsu.edu/megan/guides.html). Meteorological conditions were simulated by the WRF model. The size and color of the circles represent the installed capacity and  SO_{2}  emissions, respectively. content.title: Fig. 3. The analysis framework of the sources for energy increase in the passenger transport sector in China.
content.summary: The diagram outlines the sources of energy increase in China's passenger transport sector, detailing sub-sectors and influential factors like population and vehicle stock structure.
content.insight: The framework highlights the impact of policy measures on energy efficiency, emphasizing transportation structure adjustment and fuel economy improvements for significant energy savings.

metadata.full_json:
{"id": "img_74", "source_md": "Energy growth sources and future energy-saving pot.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Energy growth sources and future energy-saving pot\figure_3.jpg", "title": "Fig. 3. The analysis framework of the sources for energy increase in the passenger transport sector in China.", "references": ["analysis framework is proposed to consider different characteristics of traffic modes, as shown in Fig. 3. The effects of main driving factors on total passenger transport energy use are analyzed briefly. Furthermore, a specific investigation into three subsectors of passenger transport is performed in turn.", "# Energy growth sources and future energy-saving potentials in passenger transportation sector in China", "In China, energy use from passenger vehicles is increasing rapidly due to urbanization and income growth, but the contributions of transport service structure shift and energy efficiency improvement, especially future energy-saving directions are controversial and unclear because of the differences in the choices of traffic modes, which may greatly affect the analysis results and their interpretations. This study investigates the growth sources of energy use in the passenger transport sector during 1979-2017 and future energy-saving potentials by 2030 by re-examining statistical data. Results show that the transport service increase and transport structure shift led to the increase in energy use by  $665.25\%$  and  $337.22\%$ , but approximately  $14\%$  of the increased energy use was offset by energy efficiency improvements, and its effect is limited in public transportation sector, compared with private passenger vehicle sector. In public passenger transport sector, adjusting the service or volume structure may be a desirable option while in private passenger vehicle sector the policy focus is more on improving fuel efficiency and reducing average annual kilometers traveled per capita. Overall, there is still at least  $28.3\%$  of energy reduction potential by energy efficiency improvement before 2030 compared to 2017 baseline level. Potential evaluation showed that the current potential of transport service structure adjustment based on industrial planning is limited, which highlights the necessity of reform, involving promoting the sharing transportation, railway, subway and buses.", "In the last two decades, the transport sector has become a major source of energy consumption worldwide. In China, transport energy use increased from 58.63 Mtce (million tons of coal equivalent) in 1995 to 421.91 Mtce in 2017, with an annual average growth rate of nearly  $10\%$ . Energy use from passenger transport dominates total transport energy use, accounting for nearly  $60\%$  in 2017. By 2030, more than 300 million new rural residents are expected to enter the cities in China, which will have a great impact on the passenger transport energy consumption. Passengers are relatively easily guided by policy measures to change their energy-using behaviors in their daily travel, thus leading to low-carbon", "However, one of the key challenges is that the current energy statistics of passenger transportation are fragmented in China. The lack of comprehensive statistic specification causes that the data is scattered into different sectors in energy statistical system. The data does not properly cover metrics of interest for energy consumption analysis of passenger transportation. Part of the data is covered in the Transport, Storage and Post sector and Residential Consumption sector in China Energy Statistical Yearbook [3]. The former only includes the intercity public passenger transport and", "Many studies have investigated energy consumption and emissions increase in transport sector [2,5,6]. The relationship between transport energy use and socio-economic factors has also been intensively discussed using econometric models [7-11]. Various options to save energy and reduce emissions in the transport sector have been evaluated for their reduction potential, from both the technological and behavioral perspectives [1,12,13]. In transport studies, bottom-up methodology and scenario analysis were often used to study future trends, mitigation options, and reduction potential [14,15]. In particular, road transport and passenger transport have always been the hot topics for evaluating energy consumption and emissions [16,17].", "Either from an economy-wide or a sectoral perspective, the driving forces of increased energy consumption have been extensively investigated [18-20]. In the transportation sector, these studies could be summarized from two perspectives, namely, 1) the sectoral perspective, and 2) the vehicle-based perspective. From the sectoral perspective, changes in energy consumption and emission have been analyzed either for the whole transportation sector or its subsectors. Activity factors, namely Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth, population increase, and propensity to travel, were often found to be the main drivers of growing energy consumption [21-24], which is similar to the passenger transport subsector [25-27]. Other studies have focused more on micro sections of transportation. For example, on the vehicle level, the vehicle population, fuel mix, and vehicle fuel efficiency were", "With accelerating urbanization in China, passenger transport energy use has rapidly increased, from 28.78 Mtce in 1997 to 252.47 Mtce in 2017, with an annual average growth rate of  $11.47\%$  (Fig. 1). Energy use could thus be divided into three development stages. From 1978 to 1990, the intercity public passenger transport was the main traveling mode of passengers, as the urban and the private vehicle subsectors were undeveloped. From 1990 to 2002, energy consumption of the private passenger vehicles gradually caught up with that of the intercity subsector. From 2003 to 2017, the private subsector became the major source of the passenger transport energy use. The energy intensity of passenger transport decreased continually due to technological advances, and the energy intensity of the private passenger vehicles decreased rapidly from 143.68 Tce/Mp-km (ton coal equivalent/million passenger-kilometers) in 1997 to 51.38 Tce/Mp-km in 2017, due to the policy of fuel consumption limits for passenger cars [48]. This corresponds to a huge reduction in fuel consumption. Without this reduction in energy intensity, the fuel consumption in the private passenger transport sector would have increased nearly three times greater than it is today.", "(c) Modal energy intensity  
Fig. 7. Energy consumption and passenger volume share of three modes of the urban public passenger transport, 1978-2017. Data sources: China Statistical Yearbook [4]; China Urban Construction Statistical Yearbook [69]; Statistics bulletin of transportation sector development [64]; Fuel consumption limits for passenger cars, Fuel consumption evaluation methods and targets for passenger cars [48] and the authors' personal calculation.", "The data includes socio-economic activity, transport structure and energy intensity. The socio-economic activity data includes the population, the number of households and the GDP, which are from the China Statistical Yearbook [4]; the transport-related activity data includes the passenger service of the intercity public passenger transport, the passenger volume of the urban public passenger transport and the vehicle population of the private passenger vehicles, which are from 1) Passenger Kilometers of the Transport, Postal and Telecommunication Services, and Software Industry, 2) Basic Statistics on Public Transportation in Cities by Region of the Urban, Rural and Regional Development, 3) Possession of Private Vehicles of the Transport, Postal and Telecommunication Services, and Software Industry in the China Statistical Yearbook [4]. The structure data consists of the service structure of the intercity public modes, the volume structure of the urban public modes and the vehicle type structure of the private subsector, which are calculated based on the above transport-related activity data. The intensity data includes the energy intensity of the three subsectors and the average annual mileage of the private passenger vehicles, which are from the Yearbook of China Integrated transport [63], the Statistics bulletin of transportation sector development [64], the Railway Statistics [65], and the Fuel consumption limits for passenger cars [48]. The average annual mileage of the private passenger vehicles is from the survey data in the FEEI model [49].", "The GDP per capita effect was the dominant contributor to energy consumption growth and its contribution increased over time, but the contribution decreased during 1996-2000 (Figs. 5b) and 2008-2010 (Fig. 5c) due to the Asian financial crisis and the global financial crisis respectively. The shift of various modes of passenger transport (structure effect) contributed to the energy consumption decrease in the years leading up to 1990 because the passenger service share of civil aviation decreased (Fig. 5c). Since 1990, the structure effect turned from a negative value to a positive value. After 1990, the highway and civil aviation services were promoted vigorously by the State Council and Civil Aviation Administration of China [50,66]. Therefore, adjusting the transport structure in the intercity subsector, such as promoting the transfer form highway and civil aviation to railway, could be an effective way to save energy. The passenger service intensity effect provides an increasingly negative contribution to the increase in energy consumption during 1978-2017. The energy intensity of the railway, waterway, and civil aviation services decreased rapidly due to technological advancements and improved management.", "The GDP per capita effect was the dominant contributor to energy consumption growth due to rapid economic development in China. The most prominent contribution appeared during 1991-1995 and 2006-2010, with an energy consumption increase of  $275\%$  and  $195\%$ , respectively (Fig. 6b). The passenger volume structure effect was another main driver of the energy consumption growth due to the shift in the dominant mode of transport. From the late 1980s to the 1990s, the taxi industry grew rapidly, owing to the policy of open business implemented by the State Council", "The multiplication decomposition results in the three subsectors are shown in Fig. 9. Five influential factors on energy consumption can be observed and are quite similar in their effects in the intercity and urban passenger transport subsectors. The GDP per capita increase was always the dominant contributor to rises in energy consumption, but this effect was larger in the urban subsector (2.713). The passenger volume/service per GDP played a critical role in reducing energy consumption as opposed to energy intensity in the public transportation sector. In contrast, however, the private transport subsector showed that fuel efficiency improvement, decline of family size and average annual kilometers per capita offset the effect of vehicle stock growth per capita together.", "This study contributes to understanding the underlying driving factors of energy growth and exploring energy efficiency improvement opportunities in passenger transport sector in China. First, a comprehensive picture of passenger transport energy consumption trends during 1979-2017 in China is given. Main drivers of energy growth are briefly identified through re-classifying the fragmented data statistics, which, to our knowledge, are unique and original. Second, the effects of the main driving forces of increased energy consumption are investigated firstly by using a hybrid decomposition method. Third, the future energy-saving potentials of different policy pathways until 2020 and 2030 are estimated and compared involving sharing transportation, structure adjustment, energy efficiency improvement, and urban car ownership control.", "The results show that there are diversified contributors to energy consumption depending on the traffic modes utilized. On the whole, GDP growth per capita, motorization level and shifts in service structure mainly contribute to energy consumption increase in passenger transport sector during the period 1979-2017, and they cause more than 10 times increase in energy consumption, but the actual energy consumption increased just by 8.77 times because of the effect of energy efficiency improvement. For the intercity and urban public passenger transport, the GDP growth per capita, the service or volume structure change, and population growth strongly dominate the changes in energy consumption, and the effect of energy efficiency improvement is not very significant on a relative basis. In the private passenger vehicle sector, however, the situation is significantly different. Fuel efficiency improvement, decline of family size and average annual kilometers traveled per capita together offset the positive impacts from motorization level rise and vehicle type structure change. Especially, the fuel efficiency improvement has enormously contributed to energy conservation since 2010 in China.", "Outlook analysis shows that by 2030 energy conservation potential in intercity public passenger transport sector is limited (16.6%), but there are still huge energy-saving potentials in urban public passenger transport sector (54.3%) and private passenger transport sector (34.3%). They could totally lead to 31.6% of energy consumption reduction in passenger transport sector by 2030.", "In order to reduce energy use in passenger transport sector, multiple policy directions deserve attention. In China, the motorization level could be regulated by some mandatory policies, such as the vehicle license lottery in major cities, such as Beijing and Tianjin. In future, it is essential to give priority to public transport and sharing transport, such as the subway, bus, car-sharing, and bike-sharing. Particularly, first, sharing transportation deserves more attention in China because Chinese people are more likely to accept sharing and collective ideas, and there are less regulatory restrictions in China. Second, the energy intensity reduction targets up until 2020 need to be strictly enforced, especially for fuel efficiency standards for private passenger vehicles. Third, low-intensity transport modes, such as railway, waterway, rail transit, and mini vehicles, are strongly recommended to promote the low-carbon transition.", "[1] Waisman HD, Guivarch C, Lecocq F. content.title: Fig. 2. The system architecture of this study.
content.summary: The figure illustrates the system architecture used for air quality prediction, covering data collection, preprocessing, model training, and result analysis stages.
content.insight: The architecture supports the study's predictive model combining CNN and LSTM for accurate AQI forecasting across Chinese provincial capitals.

{"id": "img_23", "source_md": "Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Air quality changes in China 2013-2020_ Effectiven\figure_2.jpg", "title": "Fig. 2. The system architecture of this study.", "references": ["The Chinese mainland is composed of 33 administrative regions, however, the policies implemented by Hong Kong and Macao special administrative regions differ from the other provinces. Moreover, coal-dominated factories and enterprises are mainly located in the capital city of these provinces, which are typical epitomes of regional environmental pollution. Therefore, a total of 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland are selected. The system architecture of this study is depicted in Fig. 2.", "Considering the data features of high-dimension, multivariable, nonlinearity and temporal-spatial correlation, and with the application of Keras and TensorFlow, a predictive model combining CNN and LSTM is applied and tailored in this study. The architecture and workflows are shown in Fig. 4 and summarized as follows.", "The study obtained the data including a total of 1440 daily air quality and meteorological data (January 22, 2016, to December 31, 2019) from 31 provincial capital cities in the Chinese mainland,  $60\%$  of which are chosen as the training set,  $20\%$  as the validation set and  $20\%$  as the test set. After determining the best basic network architecture for the current prediction tasks, models are set up in units of cities, and the training set is used to train the tailored CNN-LSTM models. The accuracy indicators of the model prediction results of 31 cities, namely RMSE values averaging 22.06, models perform well overall, indicating that the applied CNN-LSTM network has good capabilities for AQI prediction (Jin et al., 2020; Mao et al., 2021).", "Alzubaidi, L., Zhang, J., Humaidi, A.J., Al-Dujaili, A., Duan, Y., Al-Shamma, O., Santamaría, J., Fadhel, M.A., Al-Amidie, M., Farhan, L., 2021. content.title: Fig. 4. Summary of the major control regulations in China since 2013. PP: power plants; IN: industry; TR: transport; DO: domestic sector; AR: agriculture.
content.summary: The table summarizes China's major control regulations for PM2.5 mitigation from 2013 to 2020, highlighting different action plans and targeted emission sectors.
content.insight: The figure illustrates the shift in China's policy framework, gradually encompassing more emission sectors and precursors to achieve substantial PM2.5 reductions nationwide, emphasizing extensive measures in key regions.

PP: power plants; IN: industry; TR: transport; DO: domestic sector; AR: agriculture.", "references": ["Fig. 4 summarizes the major action plans for  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  control implemented by the Chinese government since 2013. The policy framework shows a clear trend of gradually involving more categories of  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  precursors and emission sectors, since traditional control actions for a single pollutant or a single sector were insufficient to deliver substantial reductions in  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations as required by the Action Plan. One notable case is that the estimated  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions and sulfate concentrations inversed from satellite observations both rebounded slightly during 2010-2011 due to a rapid increase in industrial activities and less stringent controls on the iron and steel industries (Fig. 2), which surpassed the reduction benefits of  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  controls on power plants [31]. As a result, a series of stringent control measures have been implemented in the industrial polluting sector, including strengthening industrial emissions standards, phasing out small-scale high-polluting factories, phasing out outdated industrial capacities, and upgrading industrial boilers [32]. Compared with the control measures for", "As Fig. 4 indicates, control actions have been implemented nationwide, with additional efforts in several key regions. Improving air quality and mitigating  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in core megacities (e.g., Beijing and Shanghai) have been attained due to increased attention from the central government and the public. Beijing provides a success story in tackling  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution after two-decade-long efforts. In fact, the implementation of comprehensive air pollution controls in Beijing was initiated as early as the 1990s. During the 2008 Olympic Games, extensive control measures were implemented in Beijing and surrounding provinces to guarantee good air quality [41]. Ambient concentrations of CO,  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ , and  $\\mathrm{NO}_2$  continuously decreased, even as the municipal GDP, resident population, vehicle stock, and energy consumption increased significantly from 1998 to 2013. content.title: Fig. 2. Heating area and coal consumption (1997-2018).
content.summary: Fig. 2 depicts a timeline of policies related to air pollution control and energy transition from coal to cleaner alternatives in Northern China from 1963 to 2021.
content.insight: The figure illustrates the progressive implementation of domestic and foreign policies aimed at reducing coal consumption and improving air quality, culminating in the 'Winter Clean Heating Plan' for 2017-2021.

metadata.full_json:
{"id": "img_69", "source_md": "Does energy transition improve air quality_ Eviden.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\Does energy transition improve air quality_ Eviden\figure_2.jpg", "title": "Fig. 2. Heating area and coal consumption (1997-2018).", "references": ["Due to the lower temperatures in Northern China, burning coal for heating had become the most common way in which residents kept warm in winter. Since the 1950s, the Government of China provided winter heating via coal-fired boilers in the north Huai River areas. Northern cities received free unlimited heating from November 15 to March 15. Up until now, heating has not only been an important indicator of social wealth but has also become necessary for the survival of northern people in winter. According to statistical data released by the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development (MOHURD) of the People's Republic of China, the annual growth rate of the urban centralized heating area reached  $14.04\%$  during 1991-2017 (Fig. 2). At the same time, coal consumption increased under the rising demand for heating in the two decades prior to 2013. The "coal-to-gas/electricity" policy has generally altered the previous energy consumption structure. However, the rural heating energy consumption structure has largely remained unbalanced, among which coal consumption accounts for approximately  $75\%$ . Moreover, the large scattered coal heating demand has caused a series of environmental and social problems. The combustion of coal in boilers is associated with the release of air pollutants, especially  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and total suspended particles (TSP). Winter heating has also reduced people's life expectancy in northern regions of China [27]. To meet the increasing demand for heating as well as to alleviate air pollutant emissions, the National Development and Reform Commission of the People's Republic of China announced its plan for the WCHP project (covering 2017-2021).", "implementing intervention measures have seen substantial air quality improvements [7,8]. For example, the United States of America has replaced coal with natural gas in generating electric power since 1997 and has achieved  $44\%$  and  $40\%$  reduction in  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  and  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$  emissions from the 1997 levels [9]. A cleaner heating technologies program in New Zealand has led to the significant decline of PM emissions and concentrations [10]. Such policies aiming to diversify energy structures with clean and renewable energy have been successful in certain countries, which also has important implications for China [11]. In Northern China, the annual consumption of coal used for heating is approximately 400 million metric tons, nearly  $50\%$  of which is separately distributed for use in rural areas. Moreover, the combustion of scattered coal heating has caused severe haze to occur in winter [12]. Chen et al. found that the combustion of scattered coal heating has led to an approximately two-thirds increase in air pollutants during periods when heating is employed (i.e., winter) compared to periods when heating is not employed (i.e., summer) [13]. Compared to coal, clean energies such as natural gas and wind power emit less  $\mathrm{SO}_2$ ,  $\mathrm{NO}_{\mathrm{x}}$ ,  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , and  $\mathrm{PM}_{10}$ , and thus reduce human health risks from exposure to air pollutants [9]. Therefore, switching from scattered coal", "Studies on the effectiveness of energy transition have not produced a consensus among scholars. Lin and Zhu [18] found that fuel switching in "energy saving and emission reduction" (ESER) policy had failed to decrease  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations. Li and Chen [19] reported that a city that underwent coal-to-gas transition had no significant impact on reducing  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions, and this was primarily because the city focused on the replacement of industrial coal while ignoring the combustion of scattered coal heating in rural areas. Additionally, although Zhao and Luo [20] reported that a "coal-to-gas" project decreased pollutant emissions from a household sector by substituting civil urban coal consumption, they confirmed that this project had a limited effect on reducing industrial coal consumption. Liu et al. [21] adopted the GAINS model to examine the effect of coal switching strategy in Jing-Jin-Ji region, and estimated that the primary  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and  $\mathrm{SO}_2$  decreased by  $28\%$  and  $11\%$ , respectively. Wu et al. [22] evaluated the household coal-replacement program in North China and concluded that coal-to-electricity policy achieved the goal of a clean winter, while the high-quality coal replacement policy had no improvement in indoor air quality. Since the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  (smog) crisis in the winter of 2013, environmental governance in China has shifted attention to scattered coal heating in rural areas, after which a series of policies were implemented and subsequently achieved remarkable results [23]. However, official reports may have overestimated the benefits of the clean heating policy by simply attributing the decrease in air pollutant concentrations to the promotion of clean heating policies while ignoring the interference of other exogenous energy-saving and emission-reduction policies [24]. Additionally, policy impacts will vary in different regions; thus, it is necessary to identify the validity of such policy among regions. Few studies to date have focused on the evaluation of energy transition policies in China. As an important part of China's energy transition process, it must be determined that whether or not the "Winter Clean Heating Pilot" (WCHP) project improves air quality. Answering this question can offer some guidance in China's future environmental governance while also providing a quantitative reference for other coal-based, energy-consuming developing countries. It could also be beneficial to achieve the synergy between emission reduction and air quality improvement under the background of climate change.", "On the one hand, even though the WCHP project has proven to be an effective mean to control winter-derived heating emissions, the cost of this project should not be ignored. The conversion cost of "coal-to-gas/electricity" is higher and includes a subsequent increase in the cost of gas. Replacing unprocessed coal with clean coal can be used as a transitional method in the ultimate goal of energy transition for regions that experience considerable financial governmental pressure and low residential income. The government should focus on reforming the cost of electricity/gas by updating to more advanced heating technology and devices. On the other hand, since the reduction effect of the WCHP project on regional air quality varies under different development levels and resource distributions, project control should be delegated to regional governments based on local features rather than following the outdated pattern of "one-size-fits-all". In order to minimize the negative impact of clean heating policies on energy poverty and energy consumption inequality, the government should provide subsidies to clean energy more affordable to low-income groups.", "[1] Pope lii CA, Burnett RT, Thun MJ, Calle EE, Krewski D, Ito K, et al. content.title: Fig.4 Number of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas
content.summary: The figure illustrates the trend in numbers of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei from 2015 to 2020, showing a decrease over time.
content.insight: The figure suggests a decline in industrial heat sources and anomalies, reflecting effective policy implementation and industrial transformation efforts in the Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei region.

{"id": "img_223", "source_md": "一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络\figure_4.jpg", "title": "Fig.4 Number of industrial heat source objects and industrial heat anomaly points in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas", "supplement": "(a) 工业热源对象 (b) 工业热源热异常点 图4 京津冀及周边地区工业热源对象和工业热异常点数量 ", "references": ["Abstract: Aiming at the problem of insufficient quantity and spatial refinement in the extraction of industrial heat source from annual scale thermal anomaly data, a neural network industrial heat source extraction method based on temperature feature template is proposed by using VIIRS active fire data. This study took Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas as the study area, Firstly, according to the spatial aggregation characteristics of industrial heat sources, the heat source objects were divided by the OPTICS algorithm. Secondly, according to the thermal radiation characteristics of the heat sources, the temperature characteristic template of industrial heat sources and non- industrial heat sources were constructed. Finally, the BP neural network was used to extract industrial heat source objects using the temperature feature template and heat source statistical characteristics as parameters. The results show that: (1) the extraction precision of industrial heat source of the neural network algorithm of temperature feature template proposed in this paper reached  $96.31\%$ . Compared with time filtering and logistic regression methods, the extraction precision of industrial heat sources was improved by  $8.45\%$  and  $7.53\%$ , respectively; (2) From 2015 to 2020, the number of industrial heat sources in the six provinces and cities in Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas decreased by  $27.46\%$ . The number of industrial heat source objects and heat anomalies in Hebei Province decreased by  $8.06\%$  and  $7.44\%$  annually, respectively, which was the largest decrease compared with other provinces and cities. The", "concentration of industrial heat sources in Shandong and Tianjin increased by  $25.72\%$  and  $86.64\%$ , respectively, indicating that the industrial transformation and upgrade policies in the two places have achieved remarkable results; (3) Tangshan, Handan, Lvliang, and Changzhi accounted for  $31.37\%$  of the total industrial heat sources in the study area, which are the main cities in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas. The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in seven cities such as Linfen and Taiyuan was higher than those in other cities; The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in 11 cities such as Beijing and Zhoukou was lower than those in other cities; (4) From January to May 2020, the number of industrial heat anomalies in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas remained unchanged or increased compared with the same period in 2019 and 2021. The COVID-19 had no significant impact on the industrial heat source in the study area. The number of industrial heat anomalies in Wuhan in January and February 2020 decreased by more than  $66.67\%$  compared with that in the same period in 2019 and 2021, the number of industrial heat anomalies from March to May 2020 was lower than that in the same period of 2019. The COVID-19 has had a significant impact on industrial heat sources in Wuhan from January to May 2020. This study reflects the current situation and trend of industrial heat source development in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas, which provides a valuable reference for the formulation and adjustment of relevant policies such as reducing energy consumption and improving secondary industry concentration.", "Key words: industrial heat source; OPTICS algorithm; temperature feature template; neural network; VIIRS Active Fire; Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas", "(d) Slope_NFHWH  
图5各区域工业热源对象数量、工业热异常点数量、Slope_NWH值、Slope_NFHWH值  
Fig. 5 Number of industrial heat source objects, number of industrial thermal anomaly points, Slope_NWH value and Slope_NFHWH value in each region", "ing, 2020,12(12):2061. DOI:10.3390/rs12122061

content.title: Fig. 2.  PM_{2.5}  distribution at city-level in 2014 to 2020. The frequency histograms in (a)-(g) denote the frequency distribution of  PM_{2.5}  in 368 cities across mainland China. content.summary: Frequency histograms depicting PM2.5 distribution in 368 Chinese cities from 2014-2020, with WHO air quality target lines overlaid.
content.insight: Despite improvements, many cities still fail to meet WHO air quality standards, highlighting ongoing PM2.5 pollution challenges in China.

metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_179", "source_md": "Progress of Air Pollution Control in China and Its.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Progress of Air Pollution Control in China and Its\\figure_5.jpg", "title": "Fig. 5. Trend of annual average  PM_{2.5}  concentrations for the top 20 most populated cities in China from 2013 to 2018. Observed  PM_{2.5}  concentrations were obtained from the China National Environmental Monitoring Center.", "references": ["Guided by the Action Plan, many other cities in China are following Beijing to reduce ambient  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations (Fig. 5). The strategic promotion of ecological civilization has spurred stronger willingness, clearer targets, more supportive legislation and incentives, and more stringent implementation of control actions from central to local governments. All these factors work together with public engagement to achieve progress in controlling  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution and improving public health and welfare. During the Action Plan 2013-2017,  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations deceased by  $23\\%$  on average for all the municipal cities in China [46] (Fig. 5). Reductions for the three key regions were even more significant:  $40\\%$  for BTH,  $34\\%$  for YRD, and  $28\\%$  for PRD. The average  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentration in the PRD region was  $34~\\mu \\mathrm{g}\\cdot \\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ , which adhered to NAAQS for  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ . This progress has motivated some local governments to actively target more stringent limits on  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$", "(b)  \nFig. 6. (a) Trends in observed annual  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations for BTH and 74 key cities between 2013 and 2017; (b) trends expressed as 90th percentiles in maximum daily average  $8\\mathrm{hO}_3$  concentration. Shaded areas represent the 25th-75th percentile range. Data are available in Table S1 in Appendix A. Details on monthly  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentration for 74 key cities are summarized in Tables S2-S6.  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  concentrations in the BTH region and 74 cities were obtained from the China National Environmental Monitoring Center."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Present Core Findings", "Present Quantitative Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Heatmap"]}, "key_entities": ["PM2.5 concentrations", "China National Environmental Monitoring Center", "Beijing", "ecological civilization", "Action Plan"], "concise_summary": "Heatmap showing annual average PM2.5 concentrations for the top 20 most populated cities in China from 2013 to 2018, highlighting trends in air quality improvements.", "inferred_insight": "The figure illustrates significant improvements in PM2.5 concentrations due to strategic ecological campaigns and government interventions in China's major cities.", "paper_id": 26}

content.title: Fig. 5. Comparisons of Jiangsu's annual emissions from different studies. The range line represents the  95%  CIs around the central estimates of the provincial inventory by this work.
content.summary: Figure 5 compares Jiangsu's annual emission estimates with other studies, highlighting differences in emission levels and confidence intervals.
content.insight: The figure emphasizes the importance of using local emission factors for high-resolution inventories, revealing significant variations in PM10 and PM2.5 estimates due to differing dust removal rates.

metadata.keywords: ["Jiangsu", "emissions", "provincial inventory", "gaseous pollutants", "BC", "OC", "PM10", "PM2.5", "95% CIs", "emission control technologies", "dust collectors", "Monte Carlo simulation"]
metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_266", "source_md": "Quantifying the uncertainties of China's emission.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\Quantifying the uncertainties of China's emission\\figure_5.jpg", "title": "Fig. 5. Comparisons of Jiangsu's annual emissions from different studies. The range line represents the  95%  CIs around the central estimates of the provincial inventory by this work.", "references": ["Fig. 5 compares the central estimates of Jiangsu's emissions (at provincial level) by this work with those of other studies. As limited information was available for the same year as this work (2012), the results for 2010 and 2014 were included for comparisons as well. Those inventories were developed either at national scale (MEIC; Xia et al., 2016) or regional scale (Fu et al., 2013; Shanghai Research Academy of Environmental Science, SRAES). It can be seen that most estimates are within the  $95\\%$  CIs calculated in the current study for gaseous pollutants, BC and OC. Inter-annual variability could partly explain the lower estimates by SRAES and Fu et al. (2013). Different data sources (in particular the penetrations and removal rates of emission control technologies) in multiple-scale inventories also contributed to the discrepancies in emission estimates. For  $\\mathrm{PM}_{10}$  and  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ , emissions from other studies were much smaller than this work at provincial level, even out of the  $95\\%$  CIs around the central estimates. This large discrepancy came mainly from the difference in the removal rates of dust collectors used in various inventories. While all other studies applied the national/regional averages of dust removal rates based on limited test samples to calculate the emissions, the plant-specific data obtained from on-site investigations generally implied much lower removal rates for the province, leading to larger emission estimates in the provincial inventory. The comparison revealed the importance of developing and applying local emission factors on establishment of high-resolution emission inventory whenever possible.", "Fig. 4. The distributions of  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  (a) and  $\\mathrm{NO_X}$  (b) emissions from power plants in Jiangsu in 2012, using Monte-Carlo simulation. The red and black bars represent the results from the national and provincial inventories, respectively. (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)  \nFig. 5. Comparisons of Jiangsu's annual emissions from different studies. The range line represents the  $95\\%$  CIs around the central estimates of the provincial inventory by this work.", "Following our previous work (Zhao et al., 2011), Monte Carlo simulation was applied to quantify the uncertainties of national inventory by source category for the whole country and Jiangsu province. In the simulation framework, uncertainty of each parameter was characterized by probability distribution function (PDF), and the framework randomly generated values for uncertain variables over and over to simulate a model. A simulation calculated numerous scenarios of a model by repeatedly picking values from the PDFs for all the uncertain variables, and the uncertainty of target (emissions in this case) was determined according to the ranges of calculated scenarios. The PDF was fitted for parameters with adequate measurement data, using the Kolmogorov-Smirnov test (K-A test) for the goodness-of-fit  $(\\mathrm{p} = 0.05)$ . For parameters with limited measurements, and those that fail to pass the goodness-of-fit test, probability distributions must be assumed. In particular, the uncertainties of activity levels for power sector were taken from Zhao et al. (2011). Small difference was found between the annual coal consumption officially reported for the entire country and that from the sum of provincial consumption of power plants, implying good reliability of statistics for the sector (Zhao et al., 2013). Following the guideline by IPCC (2006), normal distribution was assumed and the coefficients of variation (CV, the standard deviation divided by the mean) was set at  $5\\%$  for activity data of power sector. For other industrial sources, enhanced uncertainty of coal consumption was found through comparisons between the old and updated national energy statistics, and it would lead to an apparent uncertainty of  $38\\%$  in  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions at most (Hong et al., 2017). Accordingly, CV of activity data for industrial sources other than power sector was elevated to  $20\\%$  in this work (i.e., the  $95\\%$  confidence interval (CI) was  $39.2\\%$  around the central estimate). Determination of PDFs for emission factors will be discussed in details in the following section. All of the input", "on uncertainties of Nanjing emissions by sector. As shown in Table 5, emissions and their uncertainties of industrial sector were taken from current work, and emissions from residential and transportation sectors were kept unchanged from Zhao et al. (2015). As little progress was made to improve the uncertainty analysis of city-scale emissions from the latter two sectors, the relative ranges around the central estimates for national inventory were applied (Zhao et al., 2011). For fugitive dust, lognormal distributions with a CV at  $100\\%$  was tentatively assumed to reflect the highly uncertain estimates. With 10,000 Monte-Carlo simulations, the  $95\\%$  CIs of BC to CO and OC to BC emission ratios were calculated at 0.0033–0.0133 and 0.79–2.92, with central estimates at 0.0074 and 1.39, respectively. In contrast, the \"top-down\" constraints from ground observation were 0.0084 and 1.59, respectively, i.e., they were  $14\\%$  larger than the bottom-up ratios, and within the  $95\\%$  CIs. It should be noted that uncertainty also existed in the method of \"top-down\" constraint estimation. For example, the uncertainty of BC to CO emission ratio derived from observation was estimated at  $20\\%$ , as the wet, dry deposition and chemistry of the species could not be fully understood (Wang et al., 2011; Zhao et al., 2015). Moreover, the primary OC to BC emission ratio was estimated with a semi-quantitative method, EC-tracer method. The determination of the ratio was arbitrary and unable to obtain single OC to BC ratio that represented a mixture of primary sources varying in time and space. Errors could be caused by the occasional irregular contributions from sources with a primary OC to EC ratio vastly different from the usual mix of sources (Cui et al., 2015). Therefore, the comparison between the bottom-up and top-down estimates needed to be interpreted with caution. Judged by the contribution to the variance of emission ratio, industrial CO and BC emissions were identified to be most significant to the uncertainty of primary BC to CO ratio, with the contribution to variance calculated at  $36\\%$  and  $25\\%$ , respectively. Industrial OC, fugitive OC, and industrial BC emissions were identified to be most significant to uncertainty of primary OC to BC, with the contribution to variance at  $25\\%$ ,  $23\\%$ , and  $20\\%$ , respectively. The result highlighted the importance of industrial emissions on the \"bottom-up\" emission ratio estimates."], "functional_roles": {"main_role": "Results & Findings", "sub_roles": ["Compare Experimental Performance", "Present Quantitative Results"]}, "visual_type": {"main_type": "Quantitative Charts", "sub_types": ["Bar Chart"]}, "key_entities": ["Jiangsu", "emissions", "provincial inventory", "gaseous pollutants", "BC", "OC", "PM10", "PM2.5", "95% CIs", "emission control technologies", "dust collectors", "Monte Carlo simulation"], "concise_summary": "Figure 5 compares Jiangsu's annual emission estimates with other studies, highlighting differences in emission levels and confidence intervals.", "inferred_insight": "The figure emphasizes the importance of using local emission factors for high-resolution inventories, revealing significant variations in PM10 and PM2.5 estimates due to differing dust removal rates.", "paper_id": 28}

content.title: Fig. 2. Trends in national average precipitation pH and total  SO_2  emissions between 1995 and 2017. Precipitation pH data were obtained from 74 sites with continuous observations in the Acid Rain Monitoring Networks [10]; the blue belt indicates the interval within one standard deviation of the mean. Emission data for  SO_2  were derived from the MEIC model.
content.summary: The figure illustrates trends in national average precipitation pH and total SO2 emissions in China from 1995 to 2017, showing a correlation between pH values and emissions.
content.insight: There is a strong negative correlation between SO2 emissions and precipitation pH, with significant policy impact noted during the 11th Five-Year Plan, highlighting past and ongoing efforts to control pollution.

This situation was improved during the 11th Five-Year Plan (FYP) (2006-2010), which set a concrete target to mitigate total  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions by  $10\\%$ . One notable case is that the estimated  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  emissions and sulfate concentrations inversed from satellite observations both rebounded slightly during 2010-2011 due to a rapid increase in industrial activities and less stringent controls on the iron and steel industries (Fig. 2), which surpassed the reduction benefits of  $\\mathrm{SO}_2$  controls on power plants [31]. content.title: Fig. 6. (a) Trends in observed annual  PM_{2.5}  concentrations for BTH and 74 key cities between 2013 and 2017; (b) trends expressed as 90th percentiles in maximum daily average  8hO_3  concentration. Shaded areas represent the 25th-75th percentile range. Data are available in Table S1 in Appendix A. Details on monthly  PM_{2.5}  concentration for 74 key cities are summarized in Tables S2-S6.  PM_{2.5}  and  O_3  concentrations in the BTH region and 74 cities were obtained from the China National Environmental Monitoring Center.
content.summary: The figure shows trends in PM2.5 and O3 concentrations between 2013 and 2017 in BTH and 74 key cities, highlighting a decrease in PM2.5 and an increase in O3 levels.
content.insight: Despite reductions in PM2.5, O3 pollution increased, posing challenges in simultaneous mitigation and highlighting the need for integrated pollution control strategies in China.

PM_{2.5}  and  O_3  concentrations in the BTH region and 74 cities were obtained from the China National Environmental Monitoring Center.", "references": ["Despite the substantial progress that has been made in addressing acid rain and  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  issues (Fig. 6(a)), an unwanted increase in ground-level  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  pollution is still found in many regions across China, posing the more complex challenge of simultaneous continuous mitigation of  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution with an emerging  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  issue. As  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  pollution has been steadily increasing in most parts of eastern China [47-50],  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  issues have recently gained more attention among both the public and academia in China. As illustrated in Fig. 6(b), the average 90th percentiles in daily maximum  $8\\mathrm{h}$ $\\mathrm{O}_3$  concentrations increased by  $20.1\\%$  and  $24.5\\%$  in 74 key cities and BTH, respectively, from 2013 to 2017. Furthermore, densely populated regions of eastern China were exposed to high  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  pollution, increasing the impacts of  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  on human health. The results of the GBD project [51] estimated that respiratory mortality attributable to long-term  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  exposure in adults is between 274000 and 316000 in China, equal to approximately  $25\\% -29\\%$  of the deaths attributable to  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure [16]. It is necessary to integrate  $\\mathrm{O}_3$  and  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  controls to ensure a pollution-free and healthy living environment in the development of ecological civilization in China.", "The biggest challenge of  $\\mathrm{O_3}$  pollution control is rooted in the complexity of atmospheric photochemistry formation, which depends on  $\\mathrm{O_3 - NO_x - VOC}$  sensitivity diagnosis and meteorological conditions [52,53]. A control strategy designed to reduce emissions of  $\\mathrm{NO}_x$  or VOCs for  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  control may result in an unexpected disadvantage for  $\\mathrm{O_3}$  reduction. Strong VOC-limiting systems are found in most urban areas of China [54,55], and  $\\mathrm{O_3}$  in urban areas can be enhanced if few or no VOCs are controlled simultaneously with  $\\mathrm{NO}_x$  [56]. For example, during the Action Plan from 2013 to 2017, anthropogenic emissions of  $\\mathrm{NO}_x$  were reduced by  $21\\%$  but VOCs increased by  $2\\%$  nationwide [57]. Such combinational changes in  $\\mathrm{NO}_x$ -VOC emissions might be beneficial for reducing  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  but not for  $\\mathrm{O_3}$ , as shown by the decreased  $\\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and increased  $\\mathrm{O_3}$  concentrations during the same period (Fig. 6). It is notable that the increase in  $\\mathrm{O_3}$  concentration during the Action Plan period is also attributable to variation in meteorological conditions—specifically, changing temperature and short-wave radiation. metadata.full_json: {"id": "img_221", "source_md": "一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络.md", "save_path": "C:\\liuxingyu\\multisubspace-data\\predataprocess\\paper_md\\一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络\\figure_2.jpg", "title": "Fig.2 Temperature frequency distribution of industrial and non-industrial temperature characteristic template", "supplement": "(a)工业温度特征模板 (b)非工业温度特征模板 图2 工业和非工业温度特征模板的温度频率分布 ", "references": ["Abstract: Aiming at the problem of insufficient quantity and spatial refinement in the extraction of industrial heat source from annual scale thermal anomaly data, a neural network industrial heat source extraction method based on temperature feature template is proposed by using VIIRS active fire data. This study took Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas as the study area, Firstly, according to the spatial aggregation characteristics of industrial heat sources, the heat source objects were divided by the OPTICS algorithm. Secondly, according to the thermal radiation characteristics of the heat sources, the temperature characteristic template of industrial heat sources and non- industrial heat sources were constructed. Finally, the BP neural network was used to extract industrial heat source objects using the temperature feature template and heat source statistical characteristics as parameters. The results show that: (1) the extraction precision of industrial heat source of the neural network algorithm of temperature feature template proposed in this paper reached  $96.31\\%$ . Compared with time filtering and logistic regression methods, the extraction precision of industrial heat sources was improved by  $8.45\\%$  and  $7.53\\%$ , respectively; (2) From 2015 to 2020, the number of industrial heat sources in the six provinces and cities in Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas decreased by  $27.46\\%$ . The number of industrial heat source objects and heat anomalies in Hebei Province decreased by  $8.06\\%$  and  $7.44\\%$  annually, respectively, which was the largest decrease compared with other provinces and cities. {"id": "img_228", "source_md": "一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\一种工业热源提取方法_利用温度特征模板的BP神经网络\figure_9.jpg", "title": "Fig.9 Number of thermal anomaly points from January to May", "supplement": "图9 1—5月热异常点数量 ", "references": ["Abstract: Aiming at the problem of insufficient quantity and spatial refinement in the extraction of industrial heat source from annual scale thermal anomaly data, a neural network industrial heat source extraction method based on temperature feature template is proposed by using VIIRS active fire data. This study took Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas as the study area, Firstly, according to the spatial aggregation characteristics of industrial heat sources, the heat source objects were divided by the OPTICS algorithm. Secondly, according to the thermal radiation characteristics of the heat sources, the temperature characteristic template of industrial heat sources and non- industrial heat sources were constructed. Finally, the BP neural network was used to extract industrial heat source objects using the temperature feature template and heat source statistical characteristics as parameters. The results show that: (1) the extraction precision of industrial heat source of the neural network algorithm of temperature feature template proposed in this paper reached  $96.31\%$ . Compared with time filtering and logistic regression methods, the extraction precision of industrial heat sources was improved by  $8.45\%$  and  $7.53\%$ , respectively; (2) From 2015 to 2020, the number of industrial heat sources in the six provinces and cities in Beijing-Tianjin- Hebei and its surrounding areas decreased by  $27.46\%$ . The number of industrial heat source objects and heat anomalies in Hebei Province decreased by  $8.06\%$  and  $7.44\%$  annually, respectively, which was the largest decrease compared with other provinces and cities. The", "concentration of industrial heat sources in Shandong and Tianjin increased by  $25.72\%$  and  $86.64\%$ , respectively, indicating that the industrial transformation and upgrade policies in the two places have achieved remarkable results; (3) Tangshan, Handan, Lvliang, and Changzhi accounted for  $31.37\%$  of the total industrial heat sources in the study area, which are the main cities in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas. The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in seven cities such as Linfen and Taiyuan was higher than those in other cities; The degree of industrial heat source accumulation and energy consumption in 11 cities such as Beijing and Zhoukou was lower than those in other cities; (4) From January to May 2020, the number of industrial heat anomalies in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas remained unchanged or increased compared with the same period in 2019 and 2021. The COVID-19 had no significant impact on the industrial heat source in the study area. The number of industrial heat anomalies in Wuhan in January and February 2020 decreased by more than  $66.67\%$  compared with that in the same period in 2019 and 2021, the number of industrial heat anomalies from March to May 2020 was lower than that in the same period of 2019. The COVID-19 has had a significant impact on industrial heat sources in Wuhan from January to May 2020. This study reflects the current situation and trend of industrial heat source development in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei and its surrounding areas, which provides a valuable reference for the formulation and adjustment of relevant policies such as reducing energy consumption and improving secondary industry concentration.", "(d) Slope_NFHWH

content.title: Fig. 8. Contribution of different factors to the change of  PM_{2.5}  concentrations (a) and  PM_{2.5} -attributable premature deaths (b) in 2014 to 2020 ("Clean production") denotes process control measures, "Optimize fuel structure" denotes structure control measures, "Energy consumption control" denotes source control measures, "Co-effect of C and PM" denotes the co-effect of  CO_{2}  and  PM_{2.5} , "Death rate" denotes health care level).
content.summary: Figure shows the contribution of various factors to changes in PM2.5 levels and related premature deaths in China from 2014 to 2020.
content.insight: The figure illustrates the effectiveness of measures like clean production in reducing PM2.5 and premature deaths, highlighting the need for targeted regional strategies and synergistic mitigation measures.

metadata.full_json:
{"id": "img_136", "source_md": "How the Air Clean Plan and carbon mitigation measu.md", "save_path": "C:\liuxingyu\multisubspace-data\predataprocess\paper_md\How the Air Clean Plan and carbon mitigation measu\figure_8.jpg", "title": "Fig. 8. Contribution of different factors to the change of  PM_{2.5}  concentrations (a) and  PM_{2.5} -attributable premature deaths (b) in 2014 to 2020 ("Clean production") denotes process control measures, "Optimize fuel structure" denotes structure control measures, "Energy consumption control" denotes source control measures, "Co-effect of C and PM" denotes the co-effect of  CO_{2}  and  PM_{2.5} , "Death rate" denotes health care level).", "references": ["China implemented a stringent Air Clean Plan (ACP) since 2013 to address environmental and health risks caused by ambient fine particulate matter  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ . However, the policy effectiveness of ACP and co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures to environment and health are still largely unknown. Using satellite-based  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  products produced in our previous study, concentration-response functions, and the logarithmic mean Divisia index (LMDI) method, we analyzed the spatiotemporal dynamics of premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure, and quantitatively estimated the policy benefits of ACP and carbon mitigation measures. We found the annual  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations in China decreased by  $33.65\%$ $(13.41\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3})$  from 2014 to 2020, accompanied by a decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths of 0.23 million  $(95\%$  confidence interval (CI): 0.22-0.27), indicating the huge benefits of China ACP for human health and environment. However, there were still 1.12 million  $(95\%$  CI: 0.79-1.56) premature deaths caused by the exposure of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  in mainland China in 2020. Among all ACP measures, clean production (contributed  $55.98\%$  and  $51.14\%$  to decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ) and energy consumption control (contributed  $32.58\%$  and  $29.54\%$  to decrease in  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ) made the largest contribution during the past seven years. Nevertheless, the environmental and health benefits of ACP are not fully synergistic in different regions, and the effectiveness of ACP measures reduced from 2018 to 2020. The co-effects of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  has become one of the major drivers for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths reduction since 2018, confirming the clear environment and health co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures. Our study suggests, with the saturation of clean production and source control, more targeted region-specific strategies and synergistic air pollution-carbon mitigation measures are critical to achieving the WHO's Air Quality Guideline target and the UN's Sustainable Development Goal Target in China.", "The  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure-related drivers (i.e., the drivers of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ ) avoided 0.10 million net premature deaths in the first phase (2014 to 2017) (Fig. 8b). The measures in ACP fully offset the increase in the number of premature deaths caused by the co-effect of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  as well as the population growth effect, indicating the explicit positive health effect of air quality improvements. The vulnerability factors (i.e., health care level and age structure) contributed to 0.03 million net premature deaths during 2014 to 2017. Amongst them, the improvement in health care level (decrease of baseline death rate) cannot fully offset the net deaths caused by the aging population in China, meaning that the health care level in China needs to be enhanced further. During the APWBSD, improvement in health care became a more effective driver than energy consumption control for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  emission to avoid  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable deaths, and can, thus, fully offset the effect of population aging. The population aging caused more premature deaths than economic growth, in the phase of APWBSD, which was distinct from APPCAP.", "We provide a comprehensive estimation of the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths, as well as spatiotemporal changes in these, and a quantitative assessment of the effectiveness of ACP methods in mainland China during 2014 to 2020. The two key contributions are: (1) We estimated the spatiotemporal variation in the number of deaths attributable to  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  exposure in mainland China during ACP based on the  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  product with the finest spatial resolution available to date, and elucidated the spatial discrepancies in policy implementation across China. China's recent clean air actions have achieved great benefits for human health and the environment. However, over  $98\%$  of the population still lived under the risk of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution ( $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations over  $15\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$ ), with 1.12 million ( $95\%$  CI: 0.79-1.56) premature deaths caused by  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  pollution in mainland China in 2020, accounting for  $\sim 27.05\%$  of global ambient  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$ -attributable premature deaths. In addition, the environmental and health benefits of China ACP are not fully synergistic in spatial pattern, with the greatest health benefits detected in the YRD region and the best environment benefits identified in the BTH region. To achieve the co-benefits of environment and health, specific policies need to be adopted under different conditions. (2) We quantified the environment and health co-benefits of ACP measures and carbon mitigation from 2014 to 2020. Clean production was the most effective abatement measure in China ACP, reducing net  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  concentrations and net premature deaths by  $18.35\mu \mathrm{g}\mathrm{m}^{-3}$  and 0.45 million, respectively. The co-effects of  $\mathrm{CO}_{2}$  and  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  contributed to a  $23\%$  and  $26\%$  decrease in the number of  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths and became one of the major drivers for  $\mathrm{PM}_{2.5}$  and premature deaths reduction in the APWBSD phase. This confirms the clear environment and health co-benefits of carbon mitigation measures. Meanwhile, the effectiveness of process and source control measures reduced during 2018 to 2020. With the exhaustion of end-of-pipe and energy consumption control measures, the future air clean actions should focus more on carbon abatement and energy structure optimization, especially under the background of global climate change mitigation. In addition to mitigation in industrial and energy sectors, it is also critical to explore more cost-effective approaches (e.g., agricultural ammonia mitigation) (Zhou et al., 2021; Gu et al., 2021) for pollution control in other sectors.", "Anenberg, S.C., et al., 2010. content.title: Figure 3. (a) Daily variations in China's  NO_x  emissions from coal-fire power plants around Lunar New Year 2017-2020. The dark gray shading around the blue line shows the range of daily  NO_x  emissions during 2017-2019. The light gray shading highlights the period of the Wuhan lockdown. The  x -axis represents the number of days relative to the Lunar New Year's Day. (b) Daily reductions in power plant  NO_x  emissions in Hubei and other provinces during the Wuhan lockdown in 2020 compared to 2019. (c) Spatial distribution of the reduction rates of  NO_x  emissions from power units in 2020 relative to 2019. The shades in each province indicate the relative differences in power  NO_x  emissions, and the shaded circles represent the changes in  NO_x  emissions for each power unit. Gray shading indicates no valid data.
content.summary: The figure shows variations and reductions in NO_x emissions from coal-fired power plants in China during Lunar New Year and Wuhan lockdown periods, highlighting spatial distribution and changes across provinces.
content.insight: The figure illustrates significant NO_x emission reductions during Wuhan's lockdown, emphasizing the impact of decreased industrial activities on air quality.

content.summary: Figure 10 shows monthly variations of SO2, CO, PM2.5, and CO2 emissions by sector in Asia for 2010, highlighting sector-specific patterns.
content.insight: Monthly emission patterns are influenced by sector activity rates, with increased industrial emissions in the latter half of the year and significant winter peaks in the residential sector due to heating.

content.title: Fig. 3. (a) Distribution of HC, CO and  NO_{x}  emission factors in the total vehicle fleet. (b) Probability density distribution of HC, CO and  NO_{x}  emission factors, stratified by vehicle emission standards.
content.summary: The figure shows the distribution and probability density of CO, HC, and NOx emission factors in vehicles, differentiated by emission standards in China.
content.insight: The figure highlights the significant heavy-tailed distribution of emissions, indicating that a small percentage of vehicles contribute disproportionately to total emissions. It underscores the importance of managing high emitters.

Hence, accurate estimates of air pollutant emissions of these fast-changing LDGVs are vital for air quality management, human healthcare, and ecological protection. However, this issue is poorly quantified due to insufficient reserves of timely updated LDGV emission factors, which are dependent on real-world activity levels. Here we constructed a big dataset of explicit emission profiles (e.g., emission factors and accumulated milestones) for 159,051 LDGVs based on an official I/M database by matching real-time traffic dynamics via real-world traffic monitoring (e.g., traffic volumes and speeds). Consequently, we provide robust evidence that the emission factors of these LDGVs follow a clear heavy-tailed distribution. Additionally, several approaches to reducing on-road vehicle emissions under realistic conditions were proposed from a big data perspective.
